q: What is the unit of force?

What happens to an object's acceleration if the force acting on it increases but its mass stays the same?

If the same force is applied to two objects, which one accelerates more?
o: The object with the greater mass.
The object with the smaller mass.

q: What is the mass of an object if a force of 50 N causes it to accelerate at 5 m/s²?

If the mass of an object doubles while the force stays the same, the acceleration will:
Which statement is true?
o: More force causes greater acceleration if mass stays constant.
Force and mass are measured in the same units.

q: A force of 24 N is applied to a 6 kg object. What is the acceleration?
An object accelerates at 8 m/s² when a force of 40 N is applied. What is its mass?
Which of the following would require the greatest force to accelerate?
A 10 kg object experiences a force of 100 N. If the force is increased to 200 N, the acceleration becomes:
Which change would cause the greatest increase in acceleration?
o: Increasing both force and mass equally.
